summary: clsx typosquat with postinstall remote code execution
details: Package name impersonates the popular `clsx` utility; the main module is a verbatim copy of clsx to provide cover. `package.json` declares a `postinstall` hook (`node./dist/postinstall.js`) that base64-decodes a URL stored in a misleadingly-named `HASH_KEY` constant (decodes to https://api.npoint.io/984b75c022a70cf00c39 — an anonymous JSON-hosting service), fetches `data.content` via axios, and pipes the response string into a detached `node` child process via stdin. The result is unconditional, attacker-controlled code execution on every `npm install`, with no integrity check, no version pinning, and a mutable third-party endpoint. The base64 wrapping and misleading variable name are evasion shapes; combined with the typosquat naming and remote-fetch-and-exec primitive, intent is unambiguous.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
